articles.title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 4 LIVE: Lok Sabha adjourned till noon amid Oppn. protest over NEET paper 'leak' discussion
articles.sum: Both Houses of Parliament have failed to transact any business for the first three days of the Monsoon Session, which commenced on July 20

articles.title: Bengaluru Tech Summit ropes in European partners to create global growth opportunities for startups
articles.sum: The collaborations are aimed at providing start-ups in Karnataka access to international markets, investors, innovation networks and cross-border partnerships

articles.title: Centre positive on cutting GST on tobacco exports from 40% to 28%: Yashwanth Kumar
articles.sum: The Union Government has assured the A.P. delegation that alternative incentive mechanisms will be explored to safeguard farmers’ interests

articles.title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: CJP demands Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation in response to PM Modi’s assurance of probe into paper leaks
articles.sum: Delhi Metro shuts 16 stations, including Rajiv Chowk, Patel Chowk, Delhi Gate and Central Secretariat; interchange facility available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat

articles.title: Rift in AMMA triggers fresh row over ‘power group’ in Malayalam cinema
articles.sum: Allegation by Shwetha Menon that the ‘power group’ named in Hema Committee report is working against her has triggered a fresh bout of infighting within AMMA even as her rival camp has asked her to divulge the names of those named in the controversial report

articles.title: Opposition reacts to PM Modi’s decision of fast-track courts, insists on Pradhan’s resignation
articles.sum: In a post on X, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi demanded Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation

articles.title: Sabarimala gold theft case: SIT detains former TDB president P.S. Prasanth for questioning
articles.sum: Prasanth’s detention comes nearly three weeks after SIT questioned Unnikrishnan Potti, the prime accused in the case. The move comes hours after Prasanth posted on Facebook that his arrest in the case was imminent

articles.title: Government to set up fast-track courts for paper leak cases, says PM Modi
articles.sum: “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth!” said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

articles.title: Bengaluru voices at Freedom Park: ‘Why isn’t Dharmendra Pradhan saying anything?’
articles.sum: Scores of students gathered at Freedom Park in Central Bengaluru condemning the police lathi charge on students who were marching to Parliament protesting against NEET irregularities

articles.title: 'Nothing more important than youth's future': PM Modi announces fast-track courts for paper leak cases
articles.sum: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases. This move aims to ensure swift and stringent punishment for offenders. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi criticized the Prime Minister, holding him responsible. Rahul reiterated student demands including ...

articles.title: 'Put your own house in order': India slams Pakistan over crossborder terror at UN
articles.sum: India strongly criticized Pakistan's continued use of crossborder terrorism as a state policy. Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish stated Jammu and Kashmir is an inalienable part of India. He highlighted Pakistan's illegal occupation as the sole unresolved issue concerning the region....

articles.title: PM Modi urges youth to lead vision of Viksit Bharat
articles.sum: PM Modi urges youth to lead vision of Viksit Bharat

articles.title: Crowds return to 'cockroach' protest after India police crackdown
articles.sum: Azadeh Moshiri reports from Delhi's Jantar Mantar, where the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has been protesting for education reforms.

articles.title: Why are thousands protesting for education reform?
articles.sum: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

articles.title: Diamonds and 'cab to orbit': Rocket aiming to transform India's space ambitions takes off
articles.sum: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

articles.title: The hunger-striking engineer demanding education reforms in India
articles.sum: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has a long history of challenging the status quo and refusing food to highlight his causes.

articles.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
articles.sum: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

articles.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
articles.sum: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.

articles.title: You're the one who harmed youths' future the most: Rahul Gandhi slams PM Modi
articles.sum: Youre the one who harmed youths future the most Rahul Gandhi slams PM Modi

articles.title: Adani seeks rule change to launch airline, challenge IndiGo-Air India duopoly
